mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals ongoing labor demand that supports corporate revenue expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following positive PAYEMS growth are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5.0 percent, restrictive monetary policy elevates discount rates and debt-service costs, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of months when FEDFUNDS > 5.0% during a 3-year walk-forward.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of falling retail sales (RSAFS) and rising unemployment (UNRATE) jointly signals a contraction in aggregate demand that is stronger than either signal alone, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The two-factor interaction of RSAFS and UNRATE does not significantly improve return prediction over individual factors (F-test p > 0.05) over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle identified by a rising federal funds rate, month-over-month industrial production growth predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days because strong supply signals incipient inflation and further Fed restraint.
failure_condition: During periods of rising FEDFUNDS, positive INDPRO growth does not produce negative 20-day returns in at least 55% of instances over a 3-year walk-forward.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening residential investment and forward demand for construction-related sectors, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following month-over-month HOUST declines are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in industrial production (INDPRO) over a three-month window signals expanding capacity utilization and corporate earnings power, predicting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following positive INDPRO momentum fall below baseline market returns with a t-test p-value > 0.05 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) of more than 0.3 percentage points over three months triggers a repricing of recession risk that produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 45% of triggered instances over a 3-year walk-forward.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP (GDPC1) growth slows while consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates, the stagflationary mix compresses real margins and valuations, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth decelerates and CPIAUCSL growth accelerates over prior 2 quarters
failure_condition: The interaction term between GDPC1 and CPIAUCSL does not significantly predict negative 20-day returns (t-stat > 2.0) over any 3-year rolling window.
